case_name: Osborn v Accident Compensation Corporation (Medical Mishap) [2018] NZACC 82 (31 May 2018)

summary.brief: Osborn v Accident Compensation Corporation (Medical Mishap) [2018] NZACC 82 (31 May 2018). Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: The Committee considers that this baby's problems began some considerable time before his mother was admitted to hospital.
evidence_index.speaker: Committee finding

evidence_index.text: Associate Professor Westgate considered the possibility that the decision to attempt to induce labour with syntocinon could have worsened the outcome.
evidence_index.speaker: Associate Professor Westgate

evidence_index.text: The appellant’s concern appears to be that the judgment under appeal might be seen as endorsing a proposition that personal injury suffered by a patient in a hospital or a professional healthcare environment might merely by reason of the incidental environment be entitled to compensation on the grounds of medical mishap.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: The legal approach to causation is different from the medical or scientific approach.
evidence_index.speaker: Court reasoning

evidence_index.text: Joanne was transferred to the Delivery Suite at 1900 and a syntocinon infusion was commenced shortly thereafter.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The CTG tracing in the Assessment Unit was abnormal, showing a baseline of between 150 and 160 bpm with reduced variability and no accelerations.
evidence_index.speaker: Medical record

evidence_index.text: The experts agree Cory had sustained significant damage by the time of the first fetal heart rate recording.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert consensus

evidence_index.text: Birth asphyxia, possible brain damage and complications allegedly as a result of delay in delivery is not considered to have been due to a failure by the registered health professional to observe a standard of care and skill that was reasonable in the circumstances.
evidence_index.speaker: Committee finding

evidence_index.text: The different methodology used under the legal method means the Court’s assessment of causation can differ from the expert opinion.
evidence_index.speaker: Court reasoning

evidence_index.text: Cory was delivered covered in “moderately thick” meconium and weighed 2980 grams.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Joanne Osborn, pregnant with her first child, reported reduced fetal movements and leg oedema.

focused_exam.abdomen: Engaged fetal head with fetal heart heard

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Possible pre-eclampsia and reduced fetal movements
critical_findings: Abnormal CTG with reduced variability and decelerations

reveal.result_summary: Abnormal CTG with baseline 150-160 bpm, reduced variability, no accelerations, and late variable decelerations
No structural abnormality, effaced ventricles suggesting generalized edema
reveal.decision_impact: Supports diagnosis of hypoxic ischemic encephalopathy

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with reduced fetal movements and oedema

key_events.event: Patient assessed by GP and referred to hospital

timeline_phases.name: Decision to Induce
timeline_phases.description: Decision made to attempt induction of labor

timeline_phases.name: Emergency Caesarean Section
timeline_phases.description: Caesarean section performed after failed induction

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient in hospital with abnormal CTG
decision_points.prompt: What should be the next step in management?

options.clinical_reasoning: Immediate delivery is indicated due to fetal distress

options.description: Attempt induction of labor with syntocinon
options.clinical_reasoning: Induction is inappropriate given the CTG findings

actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Induction was chosen despite CTG abnormalities

expected_action_court.description: Emergency caesarean section should have been performed
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence indicated immediate delivery was necessary

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Induction was inappropriate given the CTG findings indicating fetal distress
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Immediate caesarean section should have been performed

patient_outcome.description: Cory was born with severe brain injury and required resuscitation

key_findings: Failure to perform emergency cesarean section
Causation established - delay in delivery contributed to injury

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to perform emergency cesarean section
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Immediate delivery would have prevented further injury

factual_timeline.event: Patient presents with reduced fetal movements
Patient admitted for induction of labor

tests_performed.result: Abnormal with reduced variability and decelerations

allegations.allegation: Delay in decision for caesarean section
Failure to perform emergency cesarean section
